subject: science
text: CuO + H2 → Cu + H2O. In this reaction, the reactants that undergo oxidation and reduction respectively are
key_answer: H2 + CuO

subject: science
text: Silver nitrate turns into gray colour in the presence of sunlight because
options: ["Silver chloride decomposes to form silver.", "Silver chloride decomposes to form chlorine.", "Silver chloride undergoes oxidation.", "Silver chloride undergoes reduction."]
key_answer: Silver chloride decomposes to form silver.

subject: science
text: Identify the chemical equation in which the chemical reaction takes place in the following.
options: ["FeSO4 + Pb \u2192 PbSO4 + Fe", "ZnSO4 + Fe \u2192 FeSO4 + Zn", "2AgNO3 + Cu \u2192 Cu(NO3)2 + Ag", "PbCl2 + Cu \u2192 CuCl2 + Pb"]
key_answer: 2AgNO3 + Cu → Cu(NO3)2 + Ag

subject: science
text: When dilute hydrochloric acid is added to iron filings
options: ["Hydrogen gas and iron chloride are produced", "Chlorine gas and ferric hydroxide are produced", "No reaction takes place", "Ferrous salt and water are produced"]
key_answer: Hydrogen gas and iron chloride are produced

subject: science
text: The metal that displaces copper from copper sulphate is
key_answer: Iron

subject: science
text: What is a precipitation reaction? Give an example.
key_answer: Formation of an insoluble product from solutions; e.g., Na2SO4 + BaCl2 → BaSO4 + 2NaCl.

subject: science
text: Iron articles are to be painted. Why?
key_answer: To prevent rusting (corrosion) of iron.

subject: science
text: What is rancidity?
key_answer: Undesirable change in smell and taste of oily/fatty food due to oxidation.

subject: science
text: Zn + CO → ZnO + C. Identify the substances oxidized and reduced.
key_answer: C is oxidized; ZnO is reduced.

subject: science
text: Name the product formed when calcium oxide reacts with water.
key_answer: Calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)₂).

subject: science
text: Magnesium ribbon burns with a dazzling white flame to form magnesium oxide. Identify the type of reaction.
key_answer: Combination reaction / Oxidation reaction.

subject: science
text: List the observations that indicate a chemical reaction has taken place.
key_answer: Change of state; change of colour; evolution of gas; change in temperature.

subject: science
text: FeSO4 + Cu → CuSO4 + Fe. Is this reaction possible? Justify.
key_answer: No. Copper is less reactive than iron; it cannot displace iron from FeSO4.

subject: science
text: Which type of reaction yields quicklime from limestone? Write the equation.
key_answer: Decomposition reaction; CaCO3 → CaO + CO2.

subject: science
text: Fe2O3 + 2Al → Al2O3 + 2Fe — The above chemical reaction is an example for
key_answer: Displacement reaction

subject: science
text: Why should a magnesium ribbon be cleaned with sandpaper before burning it in air?
key_answer: To remove the oxide layer deposited on the ribbon.

subject: science
text: Suggest any two methods to prevent rancidity.
key_answer: Use antioxidants/nitrogen; store in airtight containers. (Any two)

subject: science
text: What is a combination reaction? Give an example.
key_answer: Two or more reactants form a single product; e.g., 2Mg + O2 → 2MgO.

subject: science
text: Name the brown fumes liberated when lead nitrate is heated. Write the balanced chemical equation.
key_answer: Nitrogen dioxide (NO2); 2Pb(NO3)2 → 2PbO + 4NO2 + O2.

subject: science
text: Why is Na2SO4 + BaCl2 called a double displacement reaction? Write the balanced equation.
key_answer: Ions are exchanged between reactants; Na2SO4 + BaCl2 → BaSO4 + 2NaCl.

subject: science
text: CuSO4 + X → Cu + Y. (X is Fe or Ag) a) Identify X with reason. b) Write the formula of Y.
key_answer: X: Fe, because Fe is more reactive than Ag. Y: FeSO4.

subject: science
text: An example for chemical change among the following is
options: ["Dissolving salt in water", "Tearing of paper", "Rusting of iron", "Boiling of water"]
key_answer: Rusting of iron

subject: science
text: This is not a property of a chemical reaction among the following.
options: ["Change in color", "Release of heat", "Change in shape", "Release of gas"]
key_answer: Change in shape

subject: science
text: The group of reactants among the following which react with each other to exchange ions and form a precipitate is
options: ["BaCl2 and Na2SO4", "Al2O3 and HCl", "NaOH and H2SO4", "Na2O and CO2"]
key_answer: BaCl2 and Na2SO4

subject: science
text: The ratio of hydrogen and oxygen gases released in the electrolysis of water is
key_answer: Hydrogen : Oxygen :: 2 : 1

subject: science
text: Ferrous sulphate crystals lose their green colour when heated because this compound
options: ["Breaks down into simpler compounds", "Loses water molecules", "Releases sulphur dioxide gas", "Produces brown smoke"]
key_answer: Loses water molecules

subject: science
text: What is a chemical reaction?
key_answer: A process involving rearrangement of atoms/molecules to form a new substance with different properties.

subject: science
text: What is a chemical equation?
key_answer: A symbolic representation of a chemical reaction using formulas and symbols.

subject: science
text: Manufacturers of chips flush the packets with nitrogen. Why?
key_answer: To prevent rancidity/oxidation of the contents.

subject: science
text: When sodium sulphate solution is added to barium chloride solution, which ions are responsible for the white precipitate?
key_answer: Ba²⁺ and SO₄²⁻ ions.

subject: science
text: What is a decomposition reaction? Give an example.
key_answer: A single reactant breaks down into two or more products; e.g., CaCO3 → CaO + CO2.

subject: science
text: What is a double displacement reaction? Give an example.
key_answer: Reactants exchange ions to form new compounds; Na2SO4 + BaCl2 → BaSO4 + 2NaCl.

subject: science
text: What is a redox reaction? Give an example.
key_answer: Oxidation and reduction occur simultaneously; ZnO + C → Zn + CO (C oxidized, ZnO reduced).

subject: science
text: Mention any two measures to prevent corrosion (rusting of iron).
key_answer: Painting, greasing/oil coating, anodizing, galvanization, alloying. (Any two)

subject: science
text: A shiny brown element X on heating in air becomes a black compound Y. Name X and Y.
key_answer: X: Copper; Y: Copper(II) oxide (CuO).

subject: science
text: The evolution of Oxygen and Hydrogen gases in the electrolysis of water is
key_answer: Exothermic reaction

subject: science
text: The chemical reaction that takes place when reactants exchange their ions mutually is
key_answer: Double displacement reaction

subject: science
text: The conversion of vegetable waste into compost is an example for
key_answer: Exothermic reaction

subject: science
text: The gas released at the cathode in the electrolysis of water is
key_answer: Hydrogen

subject: science
text: Chip manufacturers flush bags of chips with nitrogen gas because
options: ["To prevent corrosion", "To prevent oxidation", "To cause corrosion", "To prevent reduction"]
key_answer: To prevent oxidation

subject: science
text: Chemical equations are to be balanced. Why?
key_answer: To satisfy the law of conservation of mass.

subject: science
text: The decomposition of vegetable matter into compost and the respiration process are exothermic reactions. Why?
key_answer: Because heat is liberated in both processes.

subject: science
text: What is corrosion?
key_answer: Gradual deterioration of metals by action of air, moisture, acids, etc.

subject: science
text: What is a displacement reaction? Give an example.
key_answer: A more reactive element displaces a less reactive element; e.g., CuSO4 + Fe → FeSO4 + Cu.

subject: science
text: Why does an iron nail change its colour in copper sulphate solution? Write the equation.
key_answer: Iron displaces copper from CuSO4; CuSO4 + Fe → FeSO4 + Cu.

subject: science
text: Which coloured precipitate forms when lead nitrate and potassium iodide solutions are mixed? Name it and the reaction type.
key_answer: Yellow precipitate of lead iodide (PbI2); double displacement reaction.

subject: science
text: What are endothermic and exothermic reactions? Give an example for each.
key_answer: Endothermic absorbs heat (CaCO3 → CaO + CO2). Exothermic releases heat (CH4 + 2O2 → CO2 + 2H2O + heat).

subject: science
text: When calcium carbonate is heated, calcium oxide and carbon dioxide are produced. Write the balanced equation and state the type of reaction.
key_answer: CaCO3 → CaO + CO2; thermal decomposition.

subject: science
text: Draw the electrolysis of water apparatus and label: a) Graphite rod b) Oxygen.
key_answer: [diagram: Electrolysis of water; electrodes are graphite rods; Oxygen at anode]

subject: science
text: To 5 ml Na2SO4 solution, equal BaCl2 is added. i) Name the white precipitate. ii) Name the ions responsible. iii) Mention the type of reaction.
key_answer: i) BaSO4 ii) Ba²⁺ and SO₄²⁻ iii) Double displacement reaction.

subject: science
text: Zinc, iron, magnesium, copper strips in FeSO4 solution (tubes A–D). In which tubes does reaction occur and why? Write equations.
key_answer: In Mg and Zn tubes (C and A). Mg and Zn are more reactive than Fe. Equations: Mg + FeSO4 → MgSO4 + Fe; Zn + FeSO4 → ZnSO4 + Fe.

subject: science
text: Name the salts used in black & white photography. Write equations when exposed to sunlight.
key_answer: Silver chloride & silver bromide. 2AgCl → 2Ag + Cl2; 2AgBr → 2Ag + Br2.

subject: science
text: A solution of substance Z is used for white washing. i) Name Z ii) Molecular formula iii) Balanced equation with water.
key_answer: i) Calcium oxide ii) CaO iii) CaO + H2O → Ca(OH)2.

subject: science
text: What type of reaction takes place when an iron nail is immersed in CuSO4? Why? Write balanced equation.
key_answer: Displacement reaction. Iron displaces Cu from CuSO4. CuSO4 + Fe → FeSO4 + Cu.

subject: science
text: Write balanced equations: i) Quicklime reacts with water ii) Zinc flakes + CuSO4 iii) NaCl + AgNO3
key_answer: i) CaO + H2O → Ca(OH)2 ii) Zn + CuSO4 → ZnSO4 + Cu iii) NaCl + AgNO3 → NaNO3 + AgCl.

subject: science
text: Explain with equation: i) Oxidation ii) Reduction.
key_answer: Oxidation: 2Cu + O2 → 2CuO (addition of oxygen). Reduction: 2MgO → 2Mg + O2 (removal of oxygen).

subject: science
text: Write the balanced equations and identify the reaction type for: i) Hydrogen + oxygen → water ii) Zinc sulphate + copper → coppersulphate + zinc iii) Zinc carbonate → zinc oxide + carbon dioxide iv) Sodium chloride + silver nitrate → silver chloride + sodium nitrate.
key_answer: i) 2H2 + O2 → 2H2O (combination) ii) ZnSO4 + Cu → CuSO4 + Zn (displacement) iii) ZnCO3 → ZnO + CO2 (decomposition) iv) NaCl + AgNO3 → AgCl + NaNO3 (double displacement).

subject: science
text: Give reasons: i) Objects made of copper lose their luster when exposed to air. ii) An iron nail in copper sulphate solution slowly turns brown.
key_answer: i) Copper gets oxidized forming copper oxide/carbonate (corrosion). ii) Displacement reaction: Fe displaces Cu from CuSO4, depositing brown Cu.

subject: science
text: Reaction of BaCl2 with Al2(SO4)3 — type? Why? Balanced equation.
key_answer: Double displacement; ions exchange. 3BaCl2 + Al2(SO4)3 → 3BaSO4 + 2AlCl3.

subject: science
text: Lead nitrate is mixed with potassium iodide solution. Answer: i) Balanced equation ii) Name of precipitate iii) Colour iv) Type of reaction.
key_answer: i) Pb(NO3)2 + 2KI → PbI2 + 2KNO3 ii) Lead iodide (PbI2) iii) Yellow iv) Double displacement reaction.

subject: science
text: Write balanced equations: i) CaCO3 → CaO + CO2 ii) H2 + Cl2 → HCl iii) Mg + HCl → MgCl2 + H2
key_answer: i) CaCO3 → CaO + CO2 ii) H2 + Cl2 → 2HCl iii) Mg + 2HCl → MgCl2 + H2.

subject: science
text: Translate to balanced equations: i) H2S burns in air to give H2O & SO2 ii) BaCl2 + Al2(SO4)3 → AlCl3 + BaSO4 iii) K + H2O → KOH + H2
key_answer: i) 2H2S + 3O2 → 2H2O + 2SO2 ii) 3BaCl2 + Al2(SO4)3 → 2AlCl3 + 3BaSO4 iii) 2K + 2H2O → 2KOH + H2.

subject: science
text: Write the balanced equation for: i) Combustion of natural gas ii) Reaction of potassium with water iii) Reaction of iron with copper sulphate.
key_answer: i) CH4 + 2O2 → CO2 + 2H2O ii) 2K + 2H2O → 2KOH + H2 iii) CuSO4 + Fe → FeSO4 + Cu.

subject: science
text: Explain with an example each for decomposition by heat, light and electricity.
key_answer: Heat: CaCO3 → CaO + CO2; Light: 2AgCl → 2Ag + Cl2; Electricity: 2H2O → 2H2 + O2.

subject: science
text: Balance: i) H2 + O2 → H2O ii) Na2CO3 + HCl → NaCl + H2O + CO2 iii) N2 + H2 → NH3
key_answer: i) 2H2 + O2 → 2H2O ii) Na2CO3 + 2HCl → 2NaCl + H2O + CO2 iii) N2 + 3H2 → 2NH3.
